title: Adding Redundant Attributes
breadcrumbs: Design Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Adding Redundant Attributes In some situations, introducing an extra attribute helps to improve the performance. The attribute is used to store processing results. The situation typically occurs if the following is true: A large number of data must be scanned to establish a certain processing result The processing result is needed repeatedly The performance gain is obtained because the data is scanned only once to establish the processing result. In the following illustration, the SUM_TOTAL attribute is added to the entity ORDER to store the sum of the price of the individual order items. The number of data to be scanned is small after the first time the sum total is calculated: Figure 1. Adding redundant attributes Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
